channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard Resigns as US Director of National Intelligence
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard resigned as Director of National Intelligence, citing her husband's cancer diagnosis. Aaron Lukas will serve as acting director. Her departure comes amid reports of friction within the administration over Iran policy.

item.title: Kevin Warsh Sworn In as New Federal Reserve Chair
item.description: Kevin Warsh was sworn in as Federal Reserve Chair at a White House ceremony, replacing Jerome Powell. Warsh takes office amid rising inflation and falling consumer sentiment, with markets watching closely for signals on rate policy.

item.title: Bolivia Faces Political Crisis as Protests Escalate
item.description: President Rodrigo Paz faces mounting protests demanding his resignation just six months after taking office. Highway blockades and social unrest are threatening stability in the South American nation.

item.title: Judge Dismisses Case Against Wrongfully Deported Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: A federal judge dismissed the criminal case against Kilmar Abrego Garcia, the Maryland man wrongfully deported to El Salvador in 2025, ruling the prosecution was politically motivated.

item.title: NATO Allies Seek Clarity on US Troop Deployments in Europe
item.description: European allies expressed confusion after President Trump announced 5,000 additional troops for Poland while sending mixed signals about broader US military presence in Europe. Secretary of State Rubio sought to reassure NATO partners.

item.title: All-female Senate delegation heads to Arctic amid growing military tensions
item.description: A bipartisan group of female US senators is traveling to Greenland and Arctic nations for briefings on Arctic defense and strategic competition, highlighting growing concerns about military activity in the High North.

item.title: Rubio pushes NATO allies at key summit amid uncertainty over US commitments
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is casting doubt on NATO's relevance after key allies resisted backing Washington's positions, continuing to pressure European partners on burden-sharing.

item.title: India's billion island megaport project sharpens China's Malacca dilemma
item.description: The Great Nicobar megaport project would give India strategic monitoring capabilities near the Strait of Malacca, a critical chokepoint for Chinese trade and energy shipments.

item.title: NATO allies welcome Trump reversal on Poland troops, call for better coordination
item.description: NATO foreign ministers welcomed the US decision to station 5,000 additional troops in Poland, after days of confusion over cancelled deployments raised concerns about US reliability.

item.title: What the world gets wrong about Ukraine after four years of war
item.description: Analysis examining how international perceptions of Ukraine have shifted since Russia's full-scale invasion began, and what policymakers misunderstand about Ukraine's position and needs.

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Refinery 700km from Border
item.description: Ukrainian forces attacked a Russian oil refinery in Yaroslavl, about 435 miles from the Ukrainian border, President Zelenskiy said. It is the latest in a series of Ukrainian strikes on Russian oil facilities.

item.title: France Drafts UN Resolution on Hormuz as US Text Stalls
item.description: France has drafted a UN Security Council resolution to restore movement in the Strait of Hormuz and could submit it if conditions are right. The move comes as Washington struggles to bring a text to vote that Russia and China may see as biased against Tehran.

item.title: US Consumer Sentiment Hits Record Low on Iran War Fears
item.description: US consumer sentiment fell to a record low in May as surging gasoline prices linked to the Iran war intensified affordability concerns. The data highlights broader dissatisfaction with President Trump's handling of the economy.

item.title: Qatari Negotiating Team Arrives in Tehran for Iran Deal Push
item.description: A Qatari negotiating team has arrived in Tehran to help secure a US-Iran deal to end the war, a source said. Doha, which has worked as a mediator in the Gaza war, had until now distanced itself from a mediation role in the Iran conflict.

item.title: Putin Vows Retaliation After Ukraine Student Dorm Attack
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has promised retaliation after accusing Ukraine of carrying out a deadly attack on a student dormitory. The escalation comes amid ongoing hostilities in the conflict.

item.title: US Marine Corps Tests Helicopter-Deployed Drone Command System
item.description: Marines successfully tested controlling FPV attack drones from a UH-1Y Venom helicopter in California, extending the reach of low-cost drone systems in a new integration of aviation and unmanned capabilities.

item.title: Japan Identified as Fentanyl Smuggling Route to US, DEA Official Says
item.description: A Drug Enforcement Administration official confirmed that Japan is part of the fentanyl trafficking route to the United States, marking the first US recognition of Japan's role in the synthetic opioid supply chain.

item.title: Qatar Sends Negotiation Team to Tehran to Push US-Iran Peace Deal
item.description: A Qatari delegation arrived in Tehran in coordination with Washington to help advance negotiations aimed at ending the Iran war, joining Pakistan as key mediators in the diplomatic process.

item.title: Johor State Election Set for Three-Way Fight as Malaysian Opposition Declares Full Contest
item.description: Perikatan Nasional announced it will contest all 56 seats in Johor's upcoming state election, setting up a potential three-cornered battle with ruling coalitions PH and BN in Malaysia's most-watched state polls.

item.title: Kevin Warsh Sworn In as Federal Reserve Chair Amid Rising Inflation
item.description: Kevin Warsh was sworn in as the new Federal Reserve Chair at a pivotal moment for the US economy, facing persistent inflation and market volatility. Analysts expect no rate cuts in 2026 as inflation risks linger.

item.title: Lukashenko Vows Belarus Will Not Be Dragged Into Ukraine War
item.description: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko stated Belarus would not enter the Ukraine war, but affirmed joint defense with Russia against aggression. The statement comes amid Western concerns about Belarusian complicity in Moscows war effort.

item.title: Switzerland Expands Sanctions Against Russia and Belarus
item.description: Switzerland widened its sanctions lists against Russia and Belarus, aligning with the EUs latest measures over the war in Ukraine. The move strengthens European economic pressure on Moscow.

item.title: NATO Allies Bewildered by Trumps Sudden Troop Deployment Reversals
item.description: NATO allies expressed confusion over President Trumps contradictory announcements on US troop movements in Europe, first ordering withdrawals then reversing course. Secretary Rubio is working to reassure allies during meetings in Sweden.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as US Director of National Intelligence
item.description: Gabbard announced she will step down on June 30 to help care for her husband, who was diagnosed with bone cancer. Trump named Aaron Lukas as acting DNI chief.

item.title: Alberta to hold referendum on independence from Canada
item.description: Voters in the western province will decide next October whether to remain in Canada or pursue a binding independence referendum, amid growing separatist sentiment.
